description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a dark, marbled reddish-brown surface and evenly spaced, parallel black grooves running across its top face. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a ribbed appearance from above. The sides of the block are solid black, contrasting sharply with the textured and patterned top. The block appears dense and heavy, likely carved from a single piece of stone or engineered stone composite. Its design suggests it could serve as a decorative or functional barrier in an urban environment, such as a sidewalk divider or a low boundary marker. There are no visible signs of weathering, engravings, or additional components.
description: A rectangular, marbled reddish-brown stone block with deep, parallel black grooves on its top surface and solid black sides. The block is dense, heavy, and visually striking, suitable as an urban barrier or divider.
description_view_0: Front view showing the marbled top with horizontal black grooves and solid black front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and regularity of the parallel grooves, with the marbled top and solid black side.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the marbled top with grooves and a solid black rear face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep, evenly spaced grooves and the solid black side.
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surface appears clean and well-maintained
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
